title: Chaucerian and Other Pieces: selected texts from Skeat's 1897 edition
summary: CME00032 is a U-M electronic selection of twenty-one numbered pieces from Walter W. Skeat's 1897 Chaucerian and Other Pieces, not an encoding of one medieval work and not a complete encoding of the printed volume. It preserves the title page and printed pp. 1–145, 191–203, 205–232, 237–296, 299–326, 347–404, and 449–450. Skeat edited these heterogeneous, historically Chaucer-attributed pieces from early prints and numerous manuscripts while explicitly rejecting Chaucer's authorship. This record distinguishes the repository XML, U-M encoding, scholarly edition, its textual authorities, and later facsimiles. Complete public provider objects are cached where practical, with exact selected-work portions recorded inside each larger object and provider image terms kept separate from public-domain medieval and 1897 texts.
review_notes: The U-M display's 1894 date conflicts with the encoded title page and complete-volume catalogs, all of which establish 1897.

Complete facsimile coverage means the complete sequence exposed by the provider at review time, including binding and blank surfaces when exposed; it does not assert access to preservation masters or broaden provider reuse terms.
Skeat's source descriptions often combine transcription, collation, and consultation. Relations below preserve his explicit source roles and avoid treating every named witness as an equal base text.
MS. Digby 181 is a three-canvas published selection, so its cached bundle is complete for that digital selection but partial for the physical manuscript.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00032
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID all identify CME00032; TEMPHEAD records tag-proof review rather than bibliographic provenance.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item CME00032
entities.description: A public electronic selection from Skeat's 1897 volume. It includes twenty-one of the volume's twenty-nine numbered pieces, the source title page, and substantial editorial notes, but omits the general introduction, eight numbered pieces, the long endnotes, glossary, and indices.

entities.label: Walter W. Skeat, Chaucerian and Other Pieces (1897)
alternate_labels: Chaucerian and Other Pieces: Being a Supplement to the Complete Works of Geoffrey Chaucer
entities.description: Skeat's supplementary edition of twenty-nine pieces formerly or conventionally associated with Chaucer, with introductions, textual notes, glossary, and indices. Skeat states that none of these pieces should be accepted as Chaucer's work.

bibliographic.citation: Walter W. Skeat, ed., Chaucerian and Other Pieces: Edited, from Numerous Manuscripts (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1897), lxxxiv, 608 pp.
bibliographic.title: Chaucerian and Other Pieces: Edited, from Numerous Manuscripts
bibliographic.series: Being a Supplement to the Complete Works of Geoffrey Chaucer

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Skeat's Chaucerian and Other Pieces

notes: The scan exposes the binding and complete printed volume, including the matter omitted from CME00032.

entities.label: William Thynne's 1532 Works of Geoffrey Chaucer
entities.description: The 1532 collected Chaucer print conventionally cited by Skeat as Thynne's edition. It is the sole surviving authority for The Testament of Love and a major printed authority for many selected pieces.

entities.label: 1542 edition of the Works of Geoffrey Chaucer
entities.description: The later Thynne-associated Chaucer edition used for comparison in Skeat's Sayings Printed by Caxton. Skeat's errata corrects a heading that mistakenly assigns the Moral Balade to 1542 rather than 1532.

entities.label: John Stow's 1561 Works of Geoffrey Chaucer
entities.description: Printed authority used by Skeat for Beware of Doubleness, the warning ballade, and the Balade in Praise of Chaucer.

entities.label: Thomas Speght's 1598 Works of Geoffrey Chaucer
entities.description: The sole surviving textual authority used by Skeat for The Flower and the Leaf and a comparison source for Jack Upland.

entities.label: Early printed copy of Jack Upland at Gonville and Caius College
entities.description: The early printed copy from which Skeat says his Jack Upland text was written out. Its exact copy-level bibliographic identity remains to be reconciled with the college catalog.

holding.notes: Skeat identifies the holding institution but not a modern shelfmark in the source statement inspected.

entities.label: William Caxton's print of Anelida and Arcite with appended sayings
entities.description: The early print from which Skeat transcribed three short proverbial verse units on p. 450; Caxton does not explicitly attribute the appended sayings to Chaucer.

entities.label: Caxton print containing Scogan's Moral Balade
entities.description: An early printed authority collated by Skeat for A Moral Balade; the exact exemplar remains an open bibliographic question.

entities.label: Chepman and Myllar print of The Complaint of the Black Knight (1508)
entities.description: An early Scottish print consulted by Skeat in editing The Complaint of the Black Knight.

entities.label: Wynkyn de Worde print of The Complaint of the Black Knight
entities.description: An early printed authority consulted by Skeat for The Complaint of the Black Knight.

entities.label: British Library, Add MS 59495 (Trentham manuscript)
entities.description: John Gower presentation manuscript, circa 1397–1400; The Praise of Peace occupies ff. 5r–10v and was collated by Skeat.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS. Fairfax 16
entities.description: A major fifteenth-century English literary miscellany used extensively by Skeat. Relevant passages are Complaint ff. 20v–30r, Cuckoo ff. 35v–39v, Letter ff. 40r–47r, La Belle Dame ff. 50v–62v, Envoy ff. 147v–148r, and Beware ff. 199r–199bis v.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS. Bodl. 638
entities.description: Fifteenth-century literary manuscript used for the Complaint, Cuckoo, and Letter. The Complaint copy at ff. 1r–4v is defective because its opening, through line 467, is lost.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS. Tanner 346
entities.description: Fifteenth-century literary manuscript used for the Letter, Complaint, Cuckoo, and Envoy. The relevant passages occupy ff. 41r–59r and 97r–101v.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS. Ashmole 59
entities.description: Manuscript authority for Scogan's Moral Balade (ff. 25r–28r), the Commendation of Our Lady (ff. 39v–41r), and Beware of Doubleness (ff. 47v–49r). No complete public digital facsimile was found.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS. Arch. Selden B.24
entities.description: A major late-medieval Scottish literary manuscript. Relevant passages are Prosperity f. 119r, Complaint ff. 120v–129v, Loyalty f. 138r, Cuckoo ff. 138v–141v, and Letter ff. 211v–217r. The Complaint lacks lines 113–126 and the Cuckoo survives only through line 246.

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS. Digby 181
entities.description: Manuscript authority for the Letter at ff. 1r–6v and Complaint at ff. 31r–39r. Digital Bodleian currently exposes only three selected images, including f. 2r and two images of f. 39r.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library, MS Ff.1.6 (Findern manuscript)
entities.description: A collaborative fifteenth- and early-sixteenth-century household manuscript. Relevant old-foliation passages are Cuckoo ff. 22r–28r, Letter ff. 71r–76v, La Belle Dame ff. 117r–134v, and Good Counsel ff. 147r–150r. Its modern CUDL foliation is respectively 15r–21r, 59r–64v, 98r–115v, and 125r–128r; missing old ff. 77–80 remove the Letter's final twenty stanzas.

entities.label: Magdalene College, Pepys Library MS 2006
entities.description: A manuscript authority for The Complaint of the Black Knight at pp. 1–17. It is not included in the Pepys Library's current public digital selection.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge, MS R.3.19
entities.description: A literary manuscript used by Skeat for the Praise of Chaucer at f. 25r, Assembly of Ladies ff. 55r–65v, La Belle Dame ff. 98r–108v, and the warning ballade at ff. 207r–207v.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge, MS R.3.20
entities.description: A manuscript authority for The Letter of Cupid at manuscript pages 116–129.

entities.label: Trinity College Cambridge, MS O.9.38
entities.description: The more complete manuscript copy used to correct the warning ballade; the relevant text is on f. 28r.

entities.label: British Library, Add MS 16165
entities.description: Manuscript authority for the Complaint at ff. 190v–200v and Beware of Doubleness at ff. 252r–253v. Legacy digital images are currently unavailable.

entities.label: British Library, Add MS 34360
entities.description: Manuscript authority for The Assembly of Ladies at ff. 37r–49r. Legacy digital images are currently unavailable.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 372
entities.description: Manuscript authority for La Belle Dame sans Mercy at ff. 61r–69v. Legacy digital images are currently unavailable.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 2251
entities.description: Manuscript authority for the warning ballade at ff. 149v–150r, Good Counsel at ff. 151r–152v, and Scogan's Moral Balade at ff. 153v–156r. Legacy digital images are currently unavailable.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 7578
entities.description: Manuscript authority for Beware of Doubleness at ff. 17v–18v. Legacy digital images are currently unavailable.

entities.label: British Library, Sloane MS 1212
entities.description: A Hoccleve manuscript whose terminal vellum leaves preserve the complete Commendation of Our Lady, beginning at f. 101v according to the current handlist. The exact terminal foliation should be verified against the object.

physical_description.extent: At least 102 folios, mainly paper with initial and terminal vellum leaves

entities.label: Huntington Library, HM 744
entities.description: Hoccleve autograph manuscript containing a revised Letter of Cupid at ff. 39v–50v; Skeat calls it Ashburnham 133, its former designation.

physical_description.format: Complete exposed color facsimile, Upper board through Former spine

agents.name: The Master and Fellows of Trinity College, Cambridge

subject.description: The complete U-M electronic item, including its encoded title page and twenty-one body pieces

object.description: Title leaf and discontiguous selected pages of the 1897 volume

scope.notes: The source PB/@REF values are offset by 84 scan leaves from printed pagination; the selection omits the general introduction, eight numbered pieces, endnotes pp. 451–554, glossary pp. 555–602, and indices pp. 603–608.

object.description: Thynne's complete Testament of Love text, the sole surviving textual authority

scope.notes: Skeat checked his transcript against the original print and reconstructed the disordered third book by changing the order of six leaves.

subject.description: Selected pieces for which Skeat cites the 1532 Chaucer print
locators: Complaint of the Black Knight, pp. 245–265
La Belle Dame sans Mercy, pp. 299–326

scope.notes: The precise base/collation role differs by piece; this relation records the common printed authority without flattening those differences.

object.description: The corresponding sayings in the 1542 Chaucer edition

subject.description: Pieces for which Skeat cites Stow 1561
locators: Balade in Praise of Chaucer, p. 450

subject.description: Jack Upland comparison and the sole-source Flower and Leaf text

scope.notes: Speght is a comparison source for Jack Upland but the sole textual source for Flower and Leaf.

object.description: Jack Upland in the early Caius printed copy
Three appended verse units following Caxton's Anelida
Caxton's copy of the Moral Balade

assertion.notes: The authority is explicit in Skeat, but the exact modern bibliographic identity of the exemplar remains unresolved.

scope.notes: The Complaint copy is defective and begins only after the loss of lines 1–467.

subject.description: Moral Balade, Commendation, and Beware of Doubleness
Letter, Complaint, Cuckoo, Prosperity, and Loyalty
Letter, Good Counsel, La Belle Dame, and Cuckoo

object.description: Corresponding Findern texts under old foliation

scope.notes: Findern was directly among Skeat's manuscript authorities; its old foliation must not be confused with the modern CUDL foliation.

subject.description: Warning ballade, La Belle Dame, Assembly, and Praise of Chaucer

scope.notes: R.3.19 is Skeat's base for the warning ballade, a source for Assembly and Praise of Chaucer, and was consulted but not fully collated for La Belle Dame; those distinct roles are recorded together under the conservative consulted relation.

subject.description: Moral Balade, Good Counsel, and Warning Men

object.description: Complete Commendation on terminal vellum leaves
locators: begins f. 101v; terminal extent to verify

locators: Letter of Cupid survives at ff. 1r–6v; the public selection includes f. 2r as canvas 1

This is the complete public three-canvas selection, not a complete facsimile of MS. Digby 181.

access.access_method: Use Cambridge Digital Library or its IIIF Presentation manifest.

local_copies.label: Complete Cambridge Digital Library facsimile of MS Ff.1.6
notes: Complete 329-canvas exposed object, from cover through spine, including all extant leaves and binding surfaces exposed by CUDL.
The CME00032 cache path is a true hard link to the already verified whole-object bundle in source-cache/CME00026; it does not duplicate stored bytes.
The bundle predominantly uses the 1800-pixel IIIF profile; a few provider responses, including the final spine, resolve through their full profile as recorded in the exact inventory.
CUDL distinguishes all-rights-reserved zoom images from downloadable images and metadata under CC BY-NC 3.0; caching does not broaden those terms.

work_portion.label: CME00032 source passages in Cambridge University Library MS Ff.1.6
locators: Cuckoo and Nightingale: old ff. 22r–28r = modern ff. 15r–21r; IIIF canvases 33–45
Letter of Cupid: old ff. 71r–76v = modern ff. 59r–64v; IIIF canvases 121–132; final twenty stanzas absent because old ff. 77–80 are missing
La Belle Dame sans Mercy: old ff. 117r–134v = modern ff. 98r–115v; IIIF canvases 199–234
Ballad of Good Counsel: old ff. 147r–150r = modern ff. 125r–128r; IIIF canvases 253–259
notes: Four discontiguous runs are mapped with both old scholarly and modern CUDL foliation; the complete bundle retains the whole exposed codex.

rights.component: Encoded textual content represented by the repository XML
rights.basis: The U-M item-level statement identifies the materials as public domain and displays No Copyright–United States; the medieval texts and 1897 source edition are also out of copyright in the United States.
notes: This status concerns textual content, not trademarks, repository infrastructure, or third-party facsimile photography.

rights.component: U-M electronic text and encoded source content
rights.basis: The item-level U-M Rights/Permissions statement says that the materials are in the public domain and displays No Copyright–United States.

rights.component: 1897 edition text, introductions, notes, glossary, and arrangement
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1897; its maximum possible U.S. copyright term has expired.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan and derivatives of the 1897 volume
rights.basis: The scanned source is a public-domain 1897 edition; no separate item-level copyright claim was identified for the mechanical scan.

rights.component: Digital Bodleian manuscript photography and IIIF derivatives
rights.basis: The IIIF attribution states Photo © Bodleian Libraries and applies CC BY-NC 4.0; Digital Bodleian terms permit non-commercial reuse subject to attribution and require permission for commercial use.
notes: The medieval text remains public domain; this record concerns the photography and digital files.

rights.basis: The manifest attributes the images to the Master and Fellows of Trinity College Cambridge and supplies CC BY-NC 4.0.

rights.component: Huntington Digital Library manuscript photography and IIIF derivatives
rights.basis: The manuscript text is public domain. Huntington's rights policy says no permission is required for public-domain material, requests citation, and places copyright assessment on the researcher, but the item manifest does not supply a Creative Commons image license.
notes: Cache retention for scholarship does not substitute for an item-level publication or commercial-use determination.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Pieces historically printed with or attributed to Chaucer
editorial_practices.representation: Twenty-nine-piece scholarly supplement with explicit attribution criticism
editorial_practices.description: Skeat selected what he considered the most important pieces historically appended to the Chaucer canon while explicitly concluding that none should be accepted as Chaucer's work.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Early printed and manuscript authorities with variant, defective, and disordered texts
editorial_practices.representation: Eclectic edited texts, variant notes, square-bracket supplements, dagger-marked emendations, and source-specific introductions
editorial_practices.description: Skeat transcribed or selected a base authority for each piece, collated named prints and manuscripts where available, supplied missing words in square brackets, marked conjectural emendations with daggers, and recorded many variants and rejected readings.
editorial_practices.scope: All selected pieces, with methods and witness roles varying by piece
notes: The relations preserve named authorities without claiming that every cited manuscript was fully collated.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Book III of The Testament of Love as misbound or misordered in the sole 1532 print
editorial_practices.representation: Reordered sequence of six source leaves, with the resulting text divided into readable paragraphs
editorial_practices.description: For The Testament of Love, Skeat transcribed Thynne 1532, proofed against the original, and reconstructed the third book by rearranging six printed leaves on internal and autobiographical grounds.
editorial_practices.scope: The Testament of Love, especially Book III

editorial_practices.source_feature: Early-print distinctions and interchange among consonantal/vocalic u and v, i and j, and some inflectional y spellings
editorial_practices.representation: Consonantal u printed as v, j distinguished from i, and some suffixal y normalized to i
editorial_practices.description: Skeat explicitly normalized character distinctions in The Testament of Love while otherwise documenting many rejected source spellings.
editorial_practices.scope: The Testament of Love; it should not be generalized silently to every piece
notes: The schema's capitalization_normalization category is used for a broader graphemic normalization practice.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Early printed punctuation and undivided prose blocks
editorial_practices.description: Skeat revised punctuation and divided prose chapters into paragraphs, explicitly so for The Testament of Love.
editorial_practices.scope: Most clearly documented for The Testament of Love

editorial_practices.source_feature: Complete 1897 volume of lxxxiv + 608 pages and twenty-nine numbered pieces
editorial_practices.representation: Title page plus twenty-one numbered pieces on discontiguous printed-page runs
editorial_practices.description: The electronic item encodes the source title page and pieces I, III–V, VII–XIV, XVI, XVIII–XXI, and XXVI–XXIX. It omits the general introduction, pieces II, VI, XV, XVII, and XXII–XXV, notes pp. 451–554, glossary pp. 555–602, and indices pp. 603–608.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Skeat's local headings, source notes, variants, and page sequence within selected pieces
editorial_practices.representation: Encoded HEAD, NOTE, PB, MILESTONE, paragraph, stanza, and line structures
editorial_practices.description: Within the selected runs, the encoding retains substantial editorial apparatus and page milestones rather than presenting a bare medieval text; it therefore represents a selection from Skeat's edition, not a fresh transcription of its manuscripts.
editorial_practices.scope: Selected title page and twenty-one body pieces

evidence.citation: General Corpus CME source XML for CME00032
evidence.locator: IDG; encoded title page; BODY/DIV1[1–21]; PB, HEAD, NOTE, LG, L, and MILESTONE elements; TEMPHEAD
evidence.summary: Identifies the artifact and preserves the selected title page, twenty-one numbered pieces, printed-page markers, editorial notes, and structural markup.

evidence.citation: Local structural and content inspection of CME00032.xml
evidence.locator: Title page; all top-level BODY/DIV1 divisions; PB/@N and PB/@REF runs; element counts
evidence.summary: Verified twenty-one body pieces, 334 body page breaks, 730 body verse groups, 5,131 body verse lines, title-page inclusion, exact printed-page runs, and the absence of a conventional TEI source description or availability statement.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, CME00032
evidence.summary: Official public digital-text record, stable identifier, discovery metadata, full-text route, and item-level rights display. Its displayed 1894 date conflicts with the encoded title page and complete-volume catalogs.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan CME00032 item-level Rights/Permissions statement
evidence.locator: Rights/Permissions
evidence.summary: States that the materials are in the public domain and displays the No Copyright–United States rights statement.

evidence.citation: Walter W. Skeat, ed., Chaucerian and Other Pieces (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1897), complete volume scan
evidence.locator: title page; introductions pp. xv–lxxxii; selected texts pp. 1–450; notes pp. 451–554; errata
evidence.summary: Direct inspection establishes the 1897 bibliographic identity, twenty-nine-piece volume boundary, the twenty-one U-M selection boundaries, named source prints and manuscripts, per-piece source roles, textual defects, attributions, and Skeat's collation and normalization practices.

evidence.citation: Folger Shakespeare Library catalog record 79297, Chaucerian and Other Pieces
evidence.summary: Independent library catalog evidence for Oxford, Clarendon Press, 1897, the supplementary series title, and complete extent lxxxiv, 608 pages.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive, Chaucerian and Other Pieces, item chaucerianandoth00chauuoft
evidence.locator: complete binding and volume; CME00032 portions at scan leaves n6, n89–n233, n279–n291, n293–n320, n325–n384, n387–n414, n435–n492, n537–n538
evidence.summary: Provides the complete source-edition scan and page metadata used for the cached PDF and exact work-portion mapping.

evidence.summary: Verifies the current shelfmark, circa 1397–1400 Gower manuscript, vii + 41 folio extent, and exact Praise of Peace location. No complete BL digital object is linked.

evidence.citation: British Library, How to order collection items
evidence.summary: Provides the current route for ordering collection items for Reading Room consultation; reproduction scope and fees remain item-specific and should be confirmed with the repository.

open_questions.question: Can complete physical-object facsimiles be obtained for the named source witnesses that have no complete public digital object?
next_steps: Ask the British Library for complete-object images of Add MSS 59495, 16165, and 34360, Harley MSS 372, 2251, and 7578, and Sloane MS 1212, explicitly including covers, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, all leaves, edges, fragments, and detached material.
Ask the Bodleian for a complete physical-object facsimile of MS. Ashmole 59 and Magdalene College for Pepys MS 2006 under their respective research and reuse terms.
Record any supplied set as complete only if the repository confirms whole-object coverage; otherwise cache it as partial with exact surfaces.

open_questions.question: Will the Bodleian expose or supply a complete facsimile of MS. Digby 181 beyond the current three-image Digital Bodleian selection?
next_steps: Request a whole-object image set from Bodleian Special Collections, distinguishing it from the public three-canvas selection.
If additional images are published, verify covers, binding surfaces, all leaves, blanks, edges, fragments, and detached material before changing coverage to complete.
notes: The cached three-image bundle is complete for the provider's current digital selection but partial for the physical manuscript.

open_questions.question: What are the exact surviving copy-level identities and modern shelfmarks for Skeat's early Caius Jack Upland print, Caxton Moral Balade authority, and early Complaint prints?
next_steps: Reconcile Skeat's abbreviated source sigla against current ESTC/ISTC and holding-library records.
Ask Gonville and Caius College to identify the Jack Upland copy used by Skeat before requesting a complete-object facsimile.
Do not collapse edition-level bibliographic identity into a particular surviving exemplar without copy-level evidence.

open_questions.question: What exact terminal folios of Sloane MS 1212 contain the complete Commendation of Our Lady?
next_steps: Inspect the manuscript or a repository-supplied complete image set to reconcile Skeat's description of the terminal vellum leaves with the current handlist's f. 101v beginning.
Record the exact terminal locator without inferring it from the codex's approximate extent.

open_questions.question: Why does the U-M display assign 1894 to a source whose encoded title page and complete-volume catalogs establish 1897?
next_steps: Ask U-M whether 1894 is a migrated catalog error or an inherited series date.
Continue to use 1897 for the edition unless contrary primary evidence emerges.
